commit.message:
fix: resolve multiple frontend crash bugs and API mismatches

- Sidebar.jsx: replace `summary.title` (undefined in FastAPI TextPreview
  schema) with `summary.uploaded_filename || summary.content_preview ||
  'Untitled'` — this was causing TypeError and blank white screen on open

- SummaryForm.jsx: same fix for summary title display in the home page
  previous-summaries grid

- SummaryView.jsx: fix summarize-again response handling — endpoint returns
  SummaryResponse directly (not `{ summary: ... }`), so use `data` not
  `data.summary`; fix percentage to be sent as a URL query param (FastAPI
  treats it as a query param, not a request body); fix prop name mismatch
  where MainContent destructured `typingComplete` but prop was passed as
  `handleTypingComplete`, breaking scroll-to-bottom after typing

- InputComponent.jsx: fix error message extraction from `data.error` to
  `data?.detail` to match FastAPI error response shape

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(frontend): fix blank screen on summary page after FastAPI migration

Three bugs caused a white crash screen on /summary/:id:

1. SummaryView: isLoading initialised to false, so Summary rendered
   immediately with an empty {} object, calling formatDate(undefined)
   which throws RangeError — fixed by initialising isLoading to true
2. SummaryView: currentSummary.length === 0 always false on an object
   (undefined !== 0) — fixed with !currentSummary.id guard
3. Field name mismatch: Flask returned `text`, FastAPI returns `content`
   — updated SummaryView, Summary, and TypingSummary to use `content`
   (Summary retains `?? summary.text` fallback for safety)
4. formatDate: added null guard to prevent RangeError on invalid dates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(frontend): add SPA fallback redirect in netlify.toml

Direct navigation to React Router routes (e.g. /summary/<id>) returned
404 because Netlify tried to serve them as static files. Adding the
catch-all /* -> /index.html redirect lets React Router handle all paths.

* chore: update coverage report

* fix: reset viewMode to day when navigating between games

When React Router reuses GameViewPage for different gameId params,
viewMode state was preserved  so navigating from a game in night
phase to a new game would skip the pre-game setup and show night view.

Now resets viewMode to 'day' and hasAutoResumed to false whenever
gameId changes, ensuring each game starts in day view with hidden
character info and proper setup prompts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update coverage report

* fix: reset game view state when navigating between games

GameViewPage was reused by React Router when switching between games
in the same session, preserving stale viewMode (night/day) state.
A game in night phase would cause new games to skip pre-game setup.

Fix: Add GameViewPageWrapper in App.tsx that keys GameViewPage by
gameId, forcing a full remount on game change. This naturally resets
all local state (viewMode, dialog open states, refs) to defaults.

Also removes dead auto-resume effect  nightProgress is never
persisted to localStorage so auto-resume on page refresh was
unreachable code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update coverage report

* docs: add non-negotiable directive against lint/quality suppressions

No eslint-disable, @ts-ignore, #pragma warning disable, //nolint,
or any equivalent suppression in any language. Fix the code instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
FIX: Resolve camera spinning by properly disabling CameraFollow in FPS mode

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
- CameraFollow is a third-person camera controller that orbits around the player
- PlayerController implements FPS camera with direct mouse look (rotates player)
- When both components are enabled, they conflict over rotation control
- Result: Infinite spinning as each tries to override the other

SOLUTION:
- Added DisableCameraFollowConflicts() method to explicitly disable CameraFollow
- This ensures clean FPS mode without competing camera systems
- Updated documentation to clarify FPS-only architecture
- Code is now self-documenting about the architectural choice

TESTING:
- Build: 0 errors (pre-existing warnings only)
- Verified: PlayerController.cs compiles cleanly
- DisableCameraFollowConflicts() safely handles missing components

This is a proper fix (not just disabling a symptom) by addressing the architectural
conflict between third-person and first-person camera systems.

ENHANCED: Strengthen maze shortcut detection & prevention

CRITICAL BUG #3 - Maze Wall Generation (ENHANCED 2026-03-12)
- Previous: Added basic validation warnings
- Now: Comprehensive shortcut detection + enhanced path generation
- Impact: Eliminates potential unintended shortcuts between spawn/exit

VERIFICATION ENHANCEMENTS:
=============================

1. VerifyMazeIntegrity() - Now includes path length analysis
   * Calculates actual path length using BFS
   * Compares to direct Manhattan distance
   * Warns if path ratio < 1.5x (indicates shortcuts)
   * Critical error if path shorter than direct distance
   * Logs detailed analysis: passages%, spawn/exit marks, path ratio

2. FindActualPathLength() - New BFS pathfinding validator
   * Traces actual walkable path from spawn to exit
   * Returns cell count of shortest playable path
   * Used to detect unintended shortcuts
   * Returns worst-case estimate if path blocked

SHORTCUT PREVENTION ENHANCEMENTS:
==================================

3. CarveIndirectPath() - Strengthened waypoint generation
   * Increased waypoints: 3-4 (was 2-4)
   * Added minimum distance between waypoints (grid/4)
   * Waypoints must be far apart to force longer paths
   * Increased wall penalty: 10x (was 5x)
   * Better logging with segment distances

VALIDATION LOGIC:
=================
- Path ratio < 1.5x: WARNING (suspicious shortcut)
- Path shorter than direct: CRITICAL (shortcut confirmed)
- Passages 15-60%: OK (healthy density)
- Passages <15%: WARNING (too dense)
- Passages >60%: WARNING (too many shortcuts)

fix: varlock printenv resolves wrong positional argument (#364)

* fix: printenv command fails when run as standalone binary

The `varlock printenv MY_VAR` command was erroneously reporting

itself (`printenv`) at index 0. The fix uses `ctx.commandPath.length`

commit.message:
Fix cloud_agent merge backend: comment on PR instead of creating separate issue (#597)

* Initial plan

* fix: cloud_agent merge backend now comments on PR instead of creating new issue

The cloud agent backend for merge conflict resolution was creating a new
GitHub issue and assigning the Copilot coding agent to it. However, the
agent works on its own branch and cannot perform a proper git merge across
branches, resulting in PRs with 0 actual changes.

This changes the cloud_agent backend to post a @copilot comment directly
on the conflicting PR, asking the agent to reapply the changes on top of
the latest base branch. This approach works because the agent operates
within the PR context rather than on a separate branch.

Changes:
- core/src/state.rs: Add `comment_only` field to PendingMergeIssue
- cli/src/merge.rs: resolve_via_cloud_agent now uses comment_on_pr
- cli/src/merge.rs: promote_pending_merge_issues handles comment-only
  entries by checking PR conflict status
- Updated and added tests for the new behavior

fix(coding-agent): use UserTenantId for credential resolution (#7078)

The extension was using Subscription.TenantId (the resource tenant) to
create the AzureDeveloperCLICredential after subscription selection. For
multi-tenant/guest users, this differs from Subscription.UserTenantId
(the user access tenant), causing 'refresh token expired' errors.

This aligns the extension with how azd core resolves credentials via
SubscriptionsManager.LookupTenant(), which returns UserAccessTenantId.

Fixes #7077
